title: Automated Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Detection on Routine CT Angiography
abstract: This mock study evaluates a deep learning pipeline for automated abdominal aortic aneurysm detection using synthetic CT angiography metadata. The model identifies infrarenal aortic dilation and produces measurement candidates for radiologist review.

title: Opportunistic AAA Screening in Abdominal CT Cohorts
abstract: We describe a simulated workflow for opportunistic abdominal aortic aneurysm screening in older adults undergoing abdominal CT. The study emphasizes cohort discovery, measurement standardization, and structured reporting.

title: Synthetic DICOM Metadata for Multisite Imaging AI Validation
abstract: This paper presents a fake multisite DICOM metadata resource for testing medical imaging AI pipelines without protected health information. Scanner manufacturer, slice thickness, contrast phase, and acquisition dates are modeled for reproducible demos.

title: Atherosclerotic Plaque Burden and Aortic Diameter on Contrast CT
abstract: In a synthetic abdominal CT cohort, atherosclerotic plaque descriptors are linked with aortic diameter categories. The mock analysis demonstrates how imaging-derived biomarkers can be represented for downstream research tools.

title: Reusable Research Tools for LLM-Assisted Clinical Cohort Exploration
abstract: This demo article describes how typed tools can help language model assistants search publications, inspect clinical metadata, and compute descriptive statistics while keeping data access patterns consistent across applications.

title: Quality Control for Abdominal Aorta Measurements in CT Research
abstract: A mock quality control framework is proposed for abdominal aorta measurement in CT research. The protocol covers outer-wall measurement, orthogonal reconstruction, diameter thresholds, and adjudication of borderline cases.
